commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Skill Workshop stale-proposal reconciliation during proposal listing, extracts shared stale marking, adds focused service tests, and adds a standalone proof script.

PR surface: Source +49, Tests +68, Other +142. Total +259 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main's listSkillProposals only reads and filters the manifest, while the PR body supplies before/after terminal output showing the pending create/update cases; I did not rerun tests in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Maintainer review is needed for the read-scope lifecycle-write contract before any narrow repair path is safe.

target.title: fix(skills): reconcile stale workshop proposals when target skill exists or is missing
target.body:
## Summary

When a pending create proposal's target skill file already exists (e.g. user manually installed it after the workshop agent's apply timed out), or a pending update proposal's target skill file has been removed, the proposal stays pending indefinitely and the Skill Workshop UI shows a misleading waiting state.

## Fix

Add reconcileStaleProposals() called from listSkillProposals() to auto-mark such proposals as stale before the UI reads them:

- create proposal: if the target skill file already exists → mark stale
- update proposal: if the target skill file has been removed → mark stale

Also extract markProposalStale() as a shared helper, replacing duplicate inline stale-marking at three existing call sites. The stale transition now wraps inside withSkillProposalTargetLock() with a fresh still-pending recheck, matching the apply/revise pattern.

## Linked Issue

Closes #90388

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Skill Workshop proposal can remain shown as waiting in the UI even after the skill has already been installed manually and is visible/active in the skills list (#90388). A pending create proposal stays pending forever when its target skill already exists; similarly for pending update proposals when the target is deleted.

**Real environment tested:** Node v22.22.0, tsx v4.22.3, Linux x86_64, OpenClaw 2026.6.1 commit `1a3ce7c2a8`. Branch fix/90388-skill-workshop-proposal-reconciliation on upstream/main.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

Step 1 — standalone proof script exercising the actual workshop service code paths (`listSkillProposals` → `reconcileStaleProposals` → `markProposalStale`):

```
node --import tsx/esm scripts/proof-90421-workshop-reconciliation.mjs
```

(Script imports the real `proposeCreateSkill`, `proposeUpdateSkill`, `listSkillProposals` from `src/skills/workshop/service.ts`, sets up temp workspace/state dirs, creates pending proposals, simulates manual skill creation/deletion, and calls `listSkillProposals()` to exercise the reconciliation path.)

Step 2 — vitest workshop service test suite:

```
OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=4 pnpm test -- src/skills/workshop/service.test.ts
```

**Evidence after fix:**

Before (dead loop — no reconciliation):

Create proposal stays pending forever after manual install:

```
$ node --import tsx/esm -e "
process.env.OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR='/tmp/proof-ws/state';
const {proposeCreateSkill, listSkillProposals} = await import('./src/skills/workshop/service.js');
const {writeSkill} = await import('./src/skills/test-support/e2e-test-helpers.js');
const ws = '/tmp/proof-ws/workspace';
await fs.mkdir(ws+'/skills',{recursive:true});
const p = await proposeCreateSkill({workspaceDir:ws, name:'Test', description:'d', content:'# T'});
// manual install — skill created outside workshop
await writeSkill({dir:ws+'/skills/test', name:'test', description:'d', body:'# T'});
const before = await listSkillProposals({workspaceDir:ws});
// WITHOUT fix: status stays 'pending' forever
console.log('status before fix:', before.proposals[0]?.status);
"
status before fix: pending
```

After (with fix — reconciliation marks stale):

```
$ node --import tsx/esm scripts/proof-90421-workshop-reconciliation.mjs
=== Real Behavior Proof: Workshop Proposal Reconciliation ===
Node.js: v22.22.0
State dir:  /tmp/proof-workshop-HnUDCC/state
Workspace:  /tmp/proof-workshop-HnUDCC/workspace

--- Scenario 1: Create proposal → manual install → reconciled stale ---

Step 1: Created proposal id=auto-helper-20260605-5010b4f3fa status=pending
Step 2: Before manual install — proposal status=pending
Step 3: Manually created target skill at .../skills/auto-helper/SKILL.md
Step 4: After listSkillProposals (with reconciliation) — proposal status=stale

PASS: Create proposal reconciled to stale when target skill already exists.

--- Scenario 2: Update proposal → target deleted → reconciled stale ---

Step 1: Created update proposal id=remove-me-20260605-55fe262a88 status=pending
Step 2: Deleted target skill file
Step 3: After listSkillProposals — proposal status=stale

PASS: Update proposal reconciled to stale when target skill is missing.

--- Scenario 3: No change → stays pending ---
Step 1: Created proposal id=keep-pending-20260605-df3c317360 status=pending
Step 2: After listSkillProposals — proposal status=pending

PASS: Proposal stays pending when no reconciliation is needed.

=== All scenarios complete ===
exit code: 0
```

Vitest confirmation:

```
$ OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=4 pnpm test -- src/skills/workshop/service.test.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  21 passed (21)
   Start at  17:12:29
   Duration  9.89s
exit code: 0
```

**Observed result after fix:** The standalone proof script exercises the actual workshop service code (`listSkillProposals` → `reconcileStaleProposals` → `markProposalStale` with target lock + still-pending recheck). All 3 scenarios pass: create proposals reconciled when target skill manually installed, update proposals reconciled when target skill deleted, and proposals stay pending when no reconciliation needed. The 21 vitest tests all pass.

**What was not tested:** Live Skill Workshop UI in a running gateway with the `/skills` command. The stale transition uses the same target-lock + still-pending recheck pattern as apply/revise paths, so the reconciliation is guarded against concurrent apply races. A maintainer can use Mantis to capture visual proof: `visual task: verify Skill Workshop shows a manually installed target proposal as stale instead of waiting.`

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
target.author: chengzhichao-xydt
